row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) maintains its position as the leader with $716.9B in revenue, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B. The top two companies hold nearly 40% of the combined revenue of the top 5 companies listed.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a net income of $132.2B, significantly surpassing NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) $120.1B and widening the gap to nearly 10% between the top two companies.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of $1T from Bank of America Corporation, which ranks second with $3.4T. This substantial lead underscores JPM's massive scale and financial resources.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Total Equity of $415.3B, a significant gap from Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) which ranks second with $411.1B, indicating a close competition for the top spot.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $3.1T, indicating a substantial difference in financial leverage among the top companies.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from second-place Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B. This substantial difference underscores JPM's strong liquidity position.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, significantly outpacing Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) by 120.2B USD. The gap between AMZN and the second-place GOOGL is substantial, indicating a substantial difference in profitability between the two companies.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with an impressive Operating Income Ebit of 133.1B USD, a significant gap from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) at 130.4B USD. The top two companies are separated by a substantial 2.7B USD, highlighting Apple's dominance in this metric.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape with combined share of 43.1%, highlighting their significant influence on overall market performance.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates sector distribution by Total Assets, accounting for 64.1% of the total. Concentration in this sector is significant, with other sectors making up less than 20% combined.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates net income generation with 39.2% share, nearly doubling the second-largest sector, Communication Services, at 24.8%. This significant concentration highlights Technology's substantial influence on overall net income.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin stands out as the highest among peers, driven by its substantial revenue of $215.9B. In contrast, UNH's 2.7% net margin indicates relatively lower profitability efficiency compared to industry leaders.
row3_heatmap: MA stands out for exceptional returns, boasting a 193.5% ROE, while JPM takes the opposite end, displaying a high leverage risk with a debt/equity ratio of 11.21x.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL's 12-month cumulative return of +107.6% stands out as the highest among the listed companies, outpacing the market. Conversely, ADBE's -48.6% return is the lowest, reflecting significant market volatility. Market sentiment remains mixed, with GOOGL's exceptional performance contrasting with ADBE's decline.
